query: Our onboarding flow animations feel stiff and mechanical. The slide-in transitions use a 300ms linear ease.
expected_behavior: Replaces the linear ease with a spring configuration (tension + friction values specified)
Does not use a cubic-bezier easing curve as the replacement — requires springs
Provides specific spring constants for a 'modal slide-in' feel (e.g. tension: 200, friction: 22)
Applies the principle: digital objects have mass and friction, not just duration
Does not redesign the onboarding flow — stays surgical on replacing the easing type
